summary.en: Eclectic, historic, and slightly gritty, Capitol Hill is the city's subculture hub. Centered around Colfax Avenue—once famously dubbed the "longest, wickedest street in America"—the neighborhood blends a 24/7 lively spirit with quiet, tree-lined residential blocks.

A fascinating transition zone where upscale boutiques and elegant, tree-lined streets blend into the lively eastern stretch of Colfax Avenue, creating a perfect environment for varied urban exploration.
The grand, sweeping civic heartbeat of Denver. Here, wide flagstone sidewalks connect political landmarks, historic mansions, and a dense concentration of the city's most important fine art museums.

LoDo is where Denver’s rich pioneer and railroad history meets modern urban revival. This area is incredibly pedestrian-friendly, bustling with train commuters and locals who weave through beautifully restored Victorian buildings that now house thriving independent businesses.
An industrial-chic haven full of bright murals, craft breweries, and bustling food markets. It’s hipster-friendly, highly walkable, and represents Denver's modern artistic renaissance.
A noisy, creative, and scruffy corridor packed with vintage stores, neon signs, and some of the best dive bars in the city. This is the epicenter of Denver's local indie music scene.

description.en: A literal hole-in-the-wall joint serving huge, cheap New York-style pizza slices. There is no seating, but it offers a quintessential, fast, and highly authentic urban street slice.
One of the neighborhood's most eclectic and long-standing independent retailers. It is a treasure trove of used literature in a deeply historical neighborhood.
A legendary piano bar and historic hangout for Beat-generation writers like Allen Ginsberg and Jack Kerouac. It boasts an agile, lively piano player and a fiercely loyal local crowd.
A sprawling, lush park that is a favorite for local gatherings. It features a striking Acropolis-inspired pavilion that offers a commanding view of the downtown skyline.
A quirky, immensely popular vegetarian hangout featuring a changing roster of unusual craft brews on tap. It is prime people-watching territory and famous for its incredible meatless buffalo wings.
A hip Mexican-themed spot known for cheap, excellent food and an expansive selection of tequilas. It captures the vibrant, youthful energy of modern Colfax Avenue.
The meticulously restored Victorian home of Margaret Brown, the famous Titanic survivor. It provides a fascinating, intimate look into Colorado’s pioneer and labor-reform history.
A legendary 24-hour Greek short-order diner that is a favorite among local celebrities. It is the ultimate late-night spot for classic Greek salads and French toast.
An upscale indoor shopping mall featuring a wide array of luxury brands, department stores, and dining options. It serves as a prominent retail hub in the Cherry Creek neighborhood.
A legendary burger joint that was once featured on the Travel Channel's Man vs. Food. It is famous for a massive variety of bizarre burger toppings, including melted peanut butter and cream cheese.
A peaceful, 24-acre oasis featuring plants from around the world. It hosts Denver's first public green roof and an incredible OmniGlobe exhibit that simulates the Earth from space.
A welcoming indie coffeeshop offering great local coffee, beer, wine, and cocktails. It caters to all dietary needs with delicious home-baked vegan and gluten-free pastries.
A fantastic mid-range Indian restaurant boasting a wide range of spice levels. It is highly inclusive, offering an extensive menu of dairy-free, vegan, and gluten-free options.
A quintessential coffeehouse known for its house-made pastries. It sits on a lovely, tree-lined neighborhood block perfect for a quiet morning.
The Colfax outpost of the area's best-known bookstore. It frequently hosts author readings and educational programs in a beautiful historical setting.
A locally loved, magical toy and costume shop. It brings out the kid in everyone with its immersive, whimsical displays.
An 1883 historic home built by the Rocky Mountain News publisher. It is elegantly furnished with original turn-of-the-20th-century pieces, offering a window into Denver's past.

name.en: Center for Colorado Women's History / Byers-Evans House

description.en: A museum built entirely to house the intact collection of the pioneering abstract expressionist Clyfford Still. The architecture is designed specifically to interact with natural light to preserve fading drawings and darkening paints.
The gorgeous domed building marking the southern edge of Downtown. You can physically stand exactly one mile above sea level on its front steps.
A restaurant by celebrity chef Keegan Gerhard where delectable sweets take center stage. It proves that dessert can be the main course, though they also offer savory items.
An expansive art museum known for its collection of Native American art and its striking modern architecture. It's a cultural cornerstone of the Golden Triangle.
An upscale but welcoming cafe with a great upstairs loft. It serves locally roasted coffee and offers a fantastic vantage point from its second-floor seating.
A modern, laptop-friendly coffee shop favored by gourmets. They pour incredible artisan coffee made with a specialized Clover machine.
A brilliant hybrid bakery and deli serving Mediterranean-inspired sandwiches and great wine. The food comes from a highly active oven, and the glorious outdoor seating is perfect on a sunny day.
An urban park located where Cherry Creek and the South Platte River meet. It is a prime summertime hangout where locals cool off in the purpose-built rapids.
The "poster boy" for American train station revivals, featuring stunning seating areas, bars, and a hotel. It represents the beating heart of Denver’s transit and architectural renaissance.
An ultra-modern building hosting innovative exhibitions, public performance spaces, and a cafe. The architecture is brilliant, and it features a rooftop deck with 360-degree views of Denver.
Tucked right inside Union Station, offering excellent grab-and-go sandwiches or relaxed patio dining. Perfect for watching the constant flow of travelers while grabbing a high-quality bite.
A stunning 1930s Art Deco martini bar tucked inside the historic Oxford Hotel, reputed to serve the best martinis in Denver. It feels like stepping back in time to the post-Prohibition era.
A massive, multi-story independent bookstore offering thousands of new and used titles. It is an iconic Denver institution known for its cozy reading nooks and community vibe.
Co-founded by a former governor, this historically significant brewpub helped put Denver on the craft beer map. Aside from fresh local brews, it offers great food and lively billiard tables.
A beloved, quick-service spot specializing in authentic Chinese street food right by the station. It provides comforting, highly authentic street eats in the heart of downtown.
A museum dedicated to the immense contributions of Black pioneers in the Old West. It is housed in the relocated 19th-century home of Dr. Justina Ford, Colorado's first Black woman doctor.

name.en: Black American West Museum & Heritage Center

description.en: The celebrated microbrewery that helped make Denver an international destination for beer-lovers. You can take a firsthand tour to learn how they brew and sample their famous creations.
A beloved live music venue showcasing local rock, country, and electronica acts. It is visually striking, with huge, brightly painted Op Art murals covering the whole front of the building.
A wildly popular restaurant, cafe, and event center. It is a must-see hub for counterculture, offering poetry slams, salsa classes, and live theater.
A quirky, family-run museum housing one of the best private baseball collections in the country. It features authentic bricks, seats, and signs from classic ballparks of old.

The RiNo Art District is renowned for its vibrant and ever-changing street art and murals, transforming industrial walls into an outdoor gallery. It's a testament to Denver's modern artistic renaissance.
A trendy breakfast spot known for drawing massive weekend crowds. The inventive morning fare is incredible, and the thick, rich hot chocolate is a show-stopper.
A massive, vibrant food market perfect for grazing. It gathers a multitude of local culinary talents under one roof, ideal for food discovery.
An eclectic, long-standing music store offering thousands of new and used vinyl records, DVDs, and CDs. It is a haven for physical media collectors and music nerds.
A vibrant dessert restaurant and bakery serving up LavAzza coffee, fruit smoothies, and Inca Kola. It offers unique Peruvian treats alongside beautiful tarts and cupcakes.
A laid-back, late-night coffeehouse serving locally roasted beans and teas. Outfitted with vintage couches, it’s the perfect place to meet friends or recharge during an urban hike.
A noisy, beloved Tex-Mex institution boasting a great patio and award-winning fare. It has consistently won "Best of" local polls for years due to its authentic southwestern flavors.
An incredible piece of public art featuring a 20-foot-high dog. The massive sculpture is ingeniously constructed entirely out of metal dog tags.
A long-standing live music venue on South Broadway, known for hosting a diverse range of local and national acts. It's a key part of Denver's indie music scene.
A scruffy bar packed with character that serves as a launching pad for rising musical stars before they hit it big. It is the undisputed champion of Denver's raw, authentic local live music scene.
A 160-acre natural oasis surrounded by historic homes. It features flower gardens, crystal clear lakes, and tree-lined paths perfect for wandering.
A popular Uptown breakfast spot known for its excellent bacon fried rice and even better congee, blending Chinese and Southeast Asian flavors.
This restaurant specializes in all things biscuits, from massive biscuit sandwiches and biscuit pot pie to biscuit cinnamon rolls.
A diner and bar with a retro, 1970s Palm Springs party vibe, perfect for brunch or evening cocktails.
A specialty sandwich shop that fills one of Denver's culinary voids with its excellent porchetta.
A unique hi-fi coffee bar and record store where you can enjoy biodynamic wine by the glass while browsing vinyl.
A Latin American fusion restaurant offering an intimate dining experience with a focus on locally sourced ingredients.
A breakfast-only spot in LoHi serving creative dishes like animal-style hash browns and other flavorful plates.
A Mexican restaurant in the LoHi neighborhood with a life-altering tostada and an extensive mezcal selection.
A fast-casual Mexican spot serving breakfast and lunch, which stands out for its heritage cooking and social enterprise mission.
A restaurant specializing in pozole and mezcal, with food so good they can joke about the dish's pre-Hispanic origins.
A legendary spot in the Santa Fe Arts District where eating a green chile-drenched breakfast burrito is considered a mandatory Denver experience.
A Mediterranean-inspired restaurant where the vegan pastas are celebrated for being as satisfying as the originals they’re based on.
A Denver staple and local favorite, this spot in Platt Park serves some of the best and most beloved burgers in town.
A stylish rooftop bar located atop the Halcyon hotel in Cherry Creek, serving contemporary American fare, creative cocktails, and fresh oven-baked pizza with great views.
A major cultural institution in City Park, packed with dinosaur fossils, ancient mummies, a planetarium, and an IMAX 3-D theater.
A large urban park that offers a lakeside picnic spot and is home to the Denver Zoo and the Museum of Nature & Science.
Located in City Park, this zoo and conservation center is a popular attraction for all ages, featuring a wide variety of animals.

name.en: Kirkland Museum of Fine & Decorative Arts

description.en: Part of the Denver Art Museum complex, this museum has a renowned collection of decorative arts with a special focus on Colorado artists.
Located steps from the Denver Art Museum, this hotel doubles as a gallery with its own range of professionally displayed works of art.
One of the largest multi-venue performance centers in the world, hosting ballets, symphonies, operas, and touring Broadway shows.
Absorb the Beaux Arts aura of Union Station's Great Hall while sipping local craft beers in this retro-toned bar.
A glamorous cocktail lounge on the mezzanine of Union Station, offering a sophisticated drinking experience.
Located in Union Station and helmed by a James Beard-award-winning chef, this spot serves American cuisine and modern takes on classic comfort food.
A revitalized, pedestrian-only vein connecting Union Station to Civic Center Park, lined with shops, restaurants, and outdoor patios.
Downtown Denver’s highest open-air bar, named for its elevation of 5,430 feet, serving seasonal bites and beverages with spectacular views.
A popular local craft brewery on Platte Street that hosts fun community events and offers a wide variety of rotating beer options.
Considered the most bohemian block in town, this pedestrianized street features swanky boutiques and high-end restaurants.
A high-end Italian restaurant located in the historic and bustling Larimer Square, known for its delicious cuisine.
A celebrated fine-dining restaurant in Larimer Square offering Mediterranean-inspired dishes in an elegant setting.
A diverse epicurean food hall and boutique hotel housed in a former 1880s brick foundry, now a landmark of the RiNo Arts District.
An intimate taproom in RiNo that feels like sitting in a friend’s garage while enjoying excellent craft brews.
A unique self-care experience where you can soak in beer bath hydrotherapy, enjoy a sauna, and sample Colorado craft brews.
The home turf of the Colorado Rockies baseball team, offering spectacular views of the Rocky Mountains and stunning sunsets.
A luxurious boutique hotel in RiNo with shared spaces inspired by 17th-century French Salons and home to the legendary Death & Co bar.
A state-of-the-art concert venue in the RiNo district that hosts a wide range of local and national music acts.
An entertaining venue combining full-motion racing simulators with some of the best handmade cocktails in the city.
An edgy and alternative rooftop bar and Social Clubhouse located atop the Catbird Hotel in RiNo.
A funky shop on South Broadway's Antique Row offering a wealth of unique and fun vintage finds.
An eclectic vintage shop in the Baker District where you can hunt for priceless and unique second-hand treasures.
A massive, psychedelic, and mind-bending immersive art experience with four alien worlds created by over 350 collaborating artists.

prompt.en: Take a selfie biting into a slice that is larger than your face.
Photograph the unassuming exterior of this beloved pizza spot.
Find a book published before 1950 and photograph its vintage spine.
Capture a photo of a unique display or a stack of interesting books inside the store.

Capture a photo of one of Clyfford Still's powerful abstract paintings.
Take a photo of your feet standing directly on the step engraved with "One Mile Above Sea Level".
Capture a wide shot of the Capitol's iconic golden dome against the Denver sky.
Take a mouth-watering close-up of your beautifully plated dessert before taking a bite.

Capture a photo of a piece of art that particularly inspires you.
Take an overhead photo of the main floor from the vantage point of the upstairs loft.
Photograph a cozy corner or interesting architectural detail within the cafe.
Snap a photo of the barista operating the unique Clover brewing machine.

Take a photo of the historic 19th-century exterior of Dr. Ford's preserved home.
Photograph an exhibit highlighting the contributions of Black pioneers.
Photograph a flight of craft beers, showcasing the different colors of their brews.
Capture a photo inside the brewery, perhaps highlighting the brewing tanks or a tour group.
Take a wide shot of the vibrant Op Art mural covering the building's facade.
Capture a photo of the venue's marquee, highlighting upcoming acts.
Capture a photo of the cafe's eclectic, bohemian interior decor.
Photograph the current event schedule board, showcasing the diverse offerings.
Find and photograph the actual section of Fenway Park's "Green Monster" on display.
Capture a photo of a unique piece of baseball history from the collection.
Find your favorite mural in the district and take a photo of it.
Capture a photo of an artist at work on a new mural (if available), or a close-up of a detailed section of street art.
Capture a glorious cheese pull or syrup drip from your breakfast dish.
Photograph the lively morning crowd and colorful interior of this popular eatery.
Snap a photo capturing the bustling, vibrant atmosphere of the market's interior.

Find a vinyl record from a local Colorado band and take a picture of the album art.

Photograph a brightly colored tart or pastry next to a can of Inca Kola.
Capture a close-up of the bakery's colorful display of desserts.
Take a cozy photo of your coffee cup resting on one of the shop's vintage couches.

Snap a colorful flatlay of your chips, salsa, and Tex-Mex spread.
Photograph the lively outdoor patio area, especially if it's a sunny day.
Get a close-up macro shot of the individual dog tags making up the animal's fur.

Capture a photo of the iconic exterior or marquee of this historic music venue.
Photograph the empty stage before a show, or the crowd during a performance (if appropriate).
Photograph the most interesting band sticker you can find plastered on the walls or the front door.
Capture a photo of the stage area, ready for a live performance.
Capture a reflection of the trees or clouds in one of the park's lakes.
Photograph a vibrant section of the flower gardens.
Capture a photo that shows the contrast between a historic Capitol Hill mansion and a modern building or structure.
Take a creative selfie with the golden dome of the Colorado State Capitol in the background.
Find a street sign named after a famous writer or poet and snap a picture.
Photograph a classic, quirky, or historic sign along Colfax Avenue.
Find a beautifully maintained garden or an interesting entryway to one of the neighborhood's apartment buildings.
Take an artistic shot of your coffee or pastry from a neighborhood cafe.
Zoom in on an interesting architectural detail of a Victorian-era house, like a stained-glass window or ornate trim.
Capture a moment of relaxation or activity in one of the neighborhood's green spaces, like Cheesman Park.
Take a photo of the exterior of a local independent bookstore.
Find and photograph a historical marker or plaque that tells a story about the area.
Find a porch with a unique personality (plants, flags, furniture) and take a picture.
Look for a faded advertisement painted on the side of a brick building and capture it.
Take a selfie that you feel captures the unique vibe of this historic and eclectic neighborhood.
Find a church or home with a beautiful stained glass window and photograph it.
Capture the exterior of a classic neighborhood dive bar.
Take a photo of an eye-catching window display at a high-end Cherry Creek boutique.
Capture the vibrant colors of a classic neon sign on East Colfax Avenue, especially at dusk.
Find a piece of public art or a sculpture in Cherry Creek and take a selfie with it.
Photograph your delicious brunch or a fancy coffee from a local cafe.
Take a scenic photo along the Cherry Creek Path, capturing the water, trees, and trail.
Find an art gallery and take a picture of its inviting entrance or a piece of art visible from the window.
Find a spot where a charming old bungalow sits next to a modern new build and capture the contrast.
Photograph a lively restaurant or cafe patio scene.
Find an independent record store, bookshop, or quirky shop and snap a photo of its storefront.
Capture a photo of a beautiful floral display, either in a park, a planter, or outside a shop.
Spot a well-dressed dog out for a walk and (with permission) take its picture.
Find a local bakery or ice cream shop and photograph its most tempting treat.
Take a selfie that captures the stylish and upscale vibe of the Cherry Creek area.
Discover and photograph a hidden courtyard or tranquil public space tucked away from the main streets.
Find a bear statue or sculpture somewhere in the neighborhood and snap a photo.
Capture a dramatic angle of the Denver Art Museum or the Clyfford Still Museum's unique architecture.
Take a selfie with the City and County Building or the State Capitol as your grand backdrop.
Find an interesting public sculpture and take a creative photo of it, playing with light and shadow.
Spot a food truck and photograph its colorful design or intriguing menu.
Photograph the unique architectural lines and shapes of the Denver Central Library.
Take a photo of your coffee or snack from a cafe that fuels the neighborhood's creative energy.
Frame a shot that includes both a historic building (like the Byers-Evans House) and a modern one.
Take a wide shot of Civic Center Park, capturing the gardens, fountains, or Greek amphitheater.
Find a reflective surface (a window, a puddle) and capture a reflection of one of the area's iconic buildings.
Photograph an interesting or colorful doorway of a gallery, apartment building, or museum.
Find and photograph a street banner advertising a current museum exhibition.
Take a selfie that reflects the creative and cultural spirit of the Golden Triangle.
Take a photo of the exterior of the United States Mint building.
Capture the long shadows cast by the tall buildings or sculptures in the late afternoon sun.
Find a small patch of green or a collection of potted plants that provides a pop of nature against the urban backdrop.
Take a classic selfie under the iconic 'Union Station - Travel by Train' sign.
Capture a photo that showcases LoDo's blend of historic brick warehouses and modern glass skyscrapers in a single frame.
Take a picture of the exterior of a craft brewery or a perfectly poured pint.
Find a decorated or particularly atmospheric alleyway and snap a creative shot.
Photograph the confluence of the South Platte River and Cherry Creek, capturing the kayakers or city skyline.
Discover a faded, painted advertisement (a 'ghost sign') on the side of an old brick building.
Take a mouth-watering photo of a dish from one of LoDo's many acclaimed restaurants.
Go to one of the pedestrian bridges over the river or train tracks and capture a unique perspective of the city.
Even if it's not game day, capture the iconic exterior of Coors Field.
Use a puddle or a building's window to capture a reflection of the LoDo streetscape.
Take a selfie that tells a story about your experience in this vibrant, historic neighborhood.
Capture the grand interior of Union Station's Great Hall, with its chandeliers and soaring ceilings.
Find a street performer on the 16th Street Mall or near Union Station and capture the scene.
Photograph a bustling outdoor patio at a bar or restaurant.
Find a plaque or an architectural detail that indicates the original purpose of one of LoDo's historic buildings.
Find your favorite piece of street art or a giant mural and capture it from a creative angle.
Become part of the art! Take a selfie where you interact with a mural or street art piece.
Photograph the details of a converted warehouse, focusing on its industrial past like old brick, steel beams, or large windows.
Capture the vibrant, bustling atmosphere inside one of RiNo's popular food halls.
Take an artistic photo of a craft beer, cider, or cocktail from a local RiNo brewery or distillery.
Find an art gallery with an interesting exterior or window display and snap a photo.
The neighborhood's spirit animal is the rhinoceros. Find a depiction of a rhino in street art or a logo and photograph it.
Explore the alleyways between Larimer and Walnut streets and photograph the incredible concentration of murals.
Take a picture of the exterior of a building that houses creative studios, workshops, or unique businesses.
Find a safe spot to capture a photo of the train tracks that run through the neighborhood, with the city or mountains in the background.
Take a selfie that captures the colorful, creative, and industrial energy of RiNo.
See if you can spot an artist at work on a new mural and (from a respectful distance) capture the creative process.
Find a piece of art in an unexpected place, like on a utility box, a dumpster, or a doorway.
Photograph a lively brewery or restaurant patio full of people enjoying the Denver sun.
Capture the string lights over Larimer Street as they begin to glow at twilight.
Take a photo that captures the diverse and eclectic mix of shops on a single block of South Broadway.
Take a selfie in front of a cool vintage clothing or antique store.
Find a classic dive bar and photograph its quirky or old-school sign.
Photograph a delicious-looking pastry or colorful cake from a local bakery.
Capture a photo of the marquee of a famous music venue like the hi-dive or Herman's Hideaway.
Take a picture of the entrance to one of the neighborhood's independent record stores.
Wander off Broadway and photograph a charming, historic Baker bungalow with its classic front porch.
Look for a small, hidden mural or piece of street art in an alley or on the side of a building.
Take an artistic photo of your coffee from one of the area's many cool coffee shops.
Photograph a dish smothered in Denver's iconic green chile from a local restaurant.
Take a selfie that captures the funky, independent spirit of South Broadway.
Capture a scene from Washington Park, like the boathouse, the flower gardens, or people enjoying the outdoors.
Find a shop with unusual or eye-catching decorations out front and snap a picture.
Photograph a cool custom bike or a line of bikes parked outside a popular spot.
As evening falls, capture the glow of a neon sign from a bar or restaurant.
Capture a photo that embodies the upscale, chic vibe of Cherry Creek. Think designer storefronts, luxury cars, or perfectly manicured gardens.
East Colfax has a gritty, historic charm. Find a neon sign, a vintage storefront, or a piece of architecture that tells a story of the past.
Find a surprisingly lush and green spot—a park, a garden, or a tree-lined street—and take a selfie enjoying this urban oasis.
Take a picture of a beautifully presented plate of food, a fancy cocktail, or a decadent dessert from a local eatery.
This area is full of sculptures and public art. Find your favorite piece and strike a pose that mimics the art.
Find a spot where sleek, modern architecture stands right next to a classic, older building. Capture the contrast.
Find the most extravagant or interesting item you can see in a shop window and take a photo of it.
Capture the essence of Denver's sunny days by taking a photo of a bustling and inviting outdoor patio at a cafe or restaurant.
Seek out a hidden courtyard or alleyway that offers a quiet escape from the main streets. Photograph its unique charm.
This is a very dog-friendly area. Take a picture of a happy dog enjoying a walk or relaxing on a patio (ask the owner first!).
Use a puddle, a glass window, or a shiny surface to capture a creative reflection of the neighborhood's streetscape.
Find a bookstore or a Little Free Library and take a photo that captures the love of reading in the neighborhood.
Photograph a cup of coffee or tea from a local cafe, with the ambiance of the shop in the background.
As the sun begins to set, capture how the golden hour light transforms a building or street in the area.
Get up close and personal with the neighborhood. Take a detailed photo of an interesting texture, like old brickwork, a painted wall, or a unique paving stone.
Take a selfie with a grand government building or monument in the background, like the State Capitol or the City and County Building.
Capture the striking architecture of one of the many museums in the Golden Triangle. Focus on the bold lines and unique shapes.
Find a public sculpture or piece of outdoor art and strike a pose that interacts with it in a creative way.
Find a bench or a patch of grass in Civic Center Park and take a photo from your point of view, showing a moment of relaxation amidst the city.
Look for a historic plaque, an old cornerstone, or an intricate architectural detail on one of the older buildings and capture it up close.
Civic Center Park is a hub for food trucks. Find one with a colorful design or an interesting menu and snap a picture.
Many buildings here have a grand, symmetrical design. Find a perfect example of symmetry and capture it head-on.
The bold architecture here creates dramatic shadows. Take a photo that focuses on the interesting patterns created by light and shadow.
Take a photo that shows the mix of culture in the area – maybe a museum banner next to a government flag, or diverse groups of people enjoying the park.
Stand at the base of a tall, impressive building and take a photo looking straight up to capture its scale.
The gardens in Civic Center Park are beautifully maintained. Find the most colorful flower bed and take a vibrant close-up shot.
Find an example of modern or brutalist architecture and capture its clean lines and geometric forms.
Stop at a local cafe or deli and take an artistic shot of your drink, whether it's a latte, an iced tea, or a craft soda.
Capture the energy of the neighborhood with a photo that shows movement, like a passing bus, a cyclist, or people walking briskly.
Photograph the State Capitol's gold dome as it catches the light of the late afternoon sun.
Find a beautifully preserved brick warehouse or building and take a photo that highlights its historic texture and character.
Capture the iconic 'Travel by Train' sign at Union Station. Try to frame it in a unique way.
Use a modern glass building to capture a reflection of an old historic warehouse, showing the neighborhood's blend of old and new.
Find a spot with a view of the downtown skyline, whether from a rooftop bar or a pedestrian bridge, and take a selfie.
Explore the alleyways between the old buildings. Many are filled with string lights, ghost signs, or small murals. Capture your favorite discovery.
Take a photo of a craft beer or a fancy cocktail at one of LoDo's many bars or breweries.
LoDo is a fashionable area. Capture a photo that represents the street style of the people you see.
Find a beautifully plated dish at a local restaurant and take a mouth-watering photo.
Capture the grandeur and bustling energy inside the Great Hall of Union Station. Focus on the architecture and the people.
Take a photo that showcases the pedestrian-friendly nature of the area, like Larimer Square or the 16th Street Mall.
After dark, capture the magic of the string lights canopy over Larimer Square or the glow of Union Station.
Head towards Confluence Park and take a photo of the South Platte River with the city skyline in the background.
Find an interesting detail on a historic building—a cornice, a ghost sign, or an old window—and get a close-up shot.
Find a local ice cream shop, bakery, or candy store and photograph their most tempting treat.
Capture a photo of the free 16th Street MallRide shuttle, a train at Union Station, or a scooter zipping by.
Find the most vibrant and colorful mural you can and take a picture. RiNo is an open-air gallery, so you won't have to look far!
Capture a photo that shows the neighborhood's industrial past meeting its creative present—think a mural on an old warehouse or a modern brewery in a former factory.
Become part of the art! Find a mural and take a selfie where you look like you're interacting with the artwork.
Take a photo that captures the atmosphere of a local brewery—the fermentation tanks, the tap handles, or people enjoying a flight of beer.
Look for small, hidden pieces of art—a sticker, a small stencil, or a sculpture in an unexpected place—and photograph your discovery.
Visit a food hall like The Source or Denver Central Market and take a photo of the most delicious-looking dish you can find.
The rhino is the symbol of the neighborhood. Find a depiction of a rhino in a mural, logo, or sculpture and snap a picture.
Peer into one of the many art galleries and take a photo of an intriguing piece of art through the window.
Find a bar serving up an inventive cocktail and take an artistic photo of the drink.
Find an object or building that has been creatively repurposed, like a shipping container cafe or a silo with a mural on it.
Capture a photo of a working studio, a maker space, or a shop where local artisans create and sell their goods.
Photograph a lively outdoor patio at a brewery or restaurant, capturing the social energy of RiNo.
The nearby train tracks are part of RiNo's industrial character. Safely take a photo that incorporates the tracks into a view of the neighborhood.
Get close to a mural and take a photo that focuses on the texture of the paint on the brick or concrete wall.
RiNo is full of live music venues. Capture a photo of a band poster, a venue's marquee, or if you're lucky, a street performer.
South Broadway is famous for its vintage and antique shops. Take a photo of a fascinating object you find in a storefront window.
Capture a single photo that shows the eclectic mix of businesses on one block—a dive bar next to a boutique next to a taco shop.
Find a classic dive bar and take a photo of its old-school sign or unique exterior.
Take a selfie that captures the unique, funky character of the South Broadway streetscape behind you.
There are many great bakeries and sweet shops here. Photograph the most colorful or creative-looking pastry or dessert you can find.
Find a record store and take a photo of an album cover that catches your eye.
While not as concentrated as RiNo, SoBo has its share of street art. Find a mural and snap a pic.
Step off Broadway onto a side street in the Baker historic district and photograph a charming Victorian-era house.
Take a photo of the marquee of one of the iconic music venues in the area, like the hi-dive or Herman's Hideaway.
Capture the vibe of a local coffee shop, focusing on the decor, the baristas at work, or the cozy seating.
Find a business with a unique, retro, or handmade sign and take a photo of it.
The area has amazing Mexican food. Take a picture of a delicious-looking taco or burrito from a local spot.
Find a small park, a community garden, or a tree-lined residential street for a moment of peace. Capture the tranquility.
After sunset, capture the glow of the neon signs and storefronts that light up South Broadway.
Find a quirky or unusual detail that embodies the spirit of the neighborhood—a strange mannequin, a funny sign, or a unique decoration.
